9.raw: Niederösterreich, Austria — Zweigelt; blue-black fruit, alpine freshness, juicy and lower tannin; mid-weight; ~12.5–13.5% ABV

10.raw: Piedmont, Italy — Barbera d'Alba or d'Asti; cherry / almond, very high acid, deep colour, crunchy fruit; top producers include Vietti, Giacomo Conterno; ~14–14.5% ABV

11.raw: Beaujolais, France — Gamay; crunchy red fruit, low tannin, juicy acid; villages or cru level; carbonic notes if Nouveau-style; ~12.5–13% ABV

12.raw: Amyndeon or Naoussa, Greece — Xinomavro; tomato / olive / savory red fruit, firm acid, firm tannin, pale garnet; Alpha Estate (Amyndeon) or Ktima Dalamara (Naoussa); ~14% ABV
